authoritative_sources.title: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) United States, 2022
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau is the authoritative NAICS publisher. Its 2022 manual defines clothing and clothing accessories retailers, shoe retailers, and cross-references to apparel manufacturing, which keeps this lane tied to the named industry instead of generic retail planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Implementation Resources for Apparel and General Merchandise
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is the recognized standards body for supply-chain identification and data exchange. Its apparel and general merchandise implementation resources define the standards-based operating toolkit used for product data, RFID, floor-ready packaging, and barcode adoption.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 US Color and Size Codes
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US color and size codes are the recognized common language for product color and size identification across clothing, footwear, and textiles. They provide the item-variant normalization needed for assortment, inventory, and allocation decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: 4-5-4 Calendar
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The National Retail Federation is the leading U.S. retail trade association, and its 4-5-4 calendar is widely followed for retail sales comparability, seasonal planning cadence, and 53-week-year handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Guideline for Floor Ready Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This GS1 US apparel and general merchandise guideline names the packaging, safety, and selling-floor readiness requirements that directly affect launch timing, receipt flow, and exception handling in apparel planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guideline for Exchanging Product Images and Attributes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US's image and attribute guideline defines the product-data elements, style-color-size identifier combinations, and apparel and footwear attribute expectations that planning systems need before product can be launched or allocated safely.
used_for: style plus color plus size identifier requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Unlocking the Benefits of 2D Barcodes in Apparel and General Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This GS1 US workgroup guideline explains how 2D barcodes support serialization, country-of-origin capture, traceability, returns management, and loss-prevention workflows in apparel and general merchandise.

authoritative_sources.title: What is RFID Inventory Management?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US documents RFID as a standards-based source of real-time inventory visibility, cycle-count accuracy, and omni-channel fulfillment support, all of which materially affect apparel allocation decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Inventory visibility and omni-channel allocation review

authoritative_sources.title: Apparel and Labeling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. public authority for textile and wool labeling. Its apparel-labeling guidance defines the mandatory label evidence for fiber content, country of origin, and responsible business identity that planners must not treat as optional.
used_for: boundary against label or legal interpretation overreach

authoritative_sources.title: Care Labeling of Textile Wearing Apparel & Certain Piece Goods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC care-labeling rule is an official legal text that defines textile wearing apparel and exclusions such as footwear. This prevents the lane from overgeneralizing clothing requirements across all products in the overlay.

authoritative_sources.title: Flammable Fabrics Act (FFA)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CPSC is the authoritative U.S. safety regulator for flammability standards. Its FFA guidance defines general-wearing-apparel and children's-sleepwear testing and certification boundaries that merchandise planning cannot override.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, season or fiscal period, channel and store-cluster scope, launch or floor-set date, and the minimum tenant planning context before creating any recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Style-color-size and product-data normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize style, color, size, GTIN, image, attribute, and floor-ready records so the lane is operating on the correct apparel or footwear variant and launch configuration.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against allocatable inventory, inbound receipts, floor-ready readiness, size curves, and channel constraints while preserving source-of-truth differences across planning, POS, OMS, WMS, and RFID systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and sequencing recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only allocation, prioritization, and launch-sequencing outputs with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, and downstream impacts on channels, floor sets, and returns risk.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Compliance, authority-boundary, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When label evidence, flammability evidence, vendor readiness, or final buy and markdown authority are missing or outside lane scope, stop deterministic completion and route to the correct owner with preserved planning context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: assortment or line plans, floor-set calendars, and channel or store-cluster plans
style-color-size masters, GS1 color and size codes, GTIN or UPC, and product images and attributes
size curves, pack ratios, open-to-buy or WSSI baselines when tenant used, and markdown posture
purchase orders, vendor commitments, ex-factory and inbound dates, and floor-ready packaging requirements
POS, OMS, WMS, RFID, and inventory snapshots including on-hand, on-order, in-transit, reserved, and allocated units
returns, reverse-logistics, serialized or mis-mate handling evidence
FTC textile or wool label evidence, care-label status, country-of-origin data, and applicable CPSC flammability or certificate records
schedule or allocation recommendations, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, and compliance-gap notes
systems_of_record: RFID, serialized inventory, or EPC event system
tenant policy and approval matrix registry

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for apparel merchandise planning
organize authoritative planning, inventory, product-data, and compliance-prerequisite records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend allocation, prioritization, and schedule changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for buying, pricing, vendor-compliance, sourcing, product-compliance, and store-operations owners
escalate: missing or stale style-color-size, size-curve, inventory, floor-set, label, or flammability records
tenant policy and public baseline conflict on calendar comparability, product readiness, or compliance handling
the request requires final buy, markdown, pricing, sourcing, vendor, or product-compliance authority
systems of record materially disagree on inventory, readiness state, or ownership
refuse: final buy, markdown, pricing, vendor-commitment, or operational approval requests
care-label, origin-label, flammability, or product-compliance interpretation requests
generic SKU or demand balancing answers that ignore apparel-specific assortment, size, floor-set, or compliance context

domain_failure_modes: Treating apparel merchandise planning as generic unit balancing and ignoring style-color-size, size-curve, floor-set, or store-cluster context.
Producing deterministic allocation despite missing product-data, label-readiness, or receipt-visibility records.
Overriding floor-ready packaging, label, or flammability prerequisites to preserve a launch date.
Drifting into final buy, markdown, vendor, or sourcing authority instead of remaining recommendation-only.
Overgeneralizing apparel compliance assumptions to footwear or other categories without checking the correct product-class boundary.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific merchandise-planning artifacts and approval thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources support the apparel planning baseline, but each tenant still needs to supply its own open-to-buy, WSSI, markdown, assortment-architecture, and approval-threshold artifacts to support deterministic recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-class-specific compliance differences across apparel, textiles, and footwear
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: FTC and CPSC sources establish the public baseline, but exact product obligations still vary by class. This package treats product-specific compliance gaps as runtime prerequisites and escalates rather than guessing.

notes: Authoritative source records for the lane are the tenant's designated merchandise-planning, PLM or PIM, ERP, POS, OMS, WMS, RFID, vendor-compliance, and approval systems constrained by the public baseline above.
The lane stops before final buy, markdown, pricing, vendor-commitment, sourcing, care-label, or flammability signoff decisions even when sales or launch pressure is high.

No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for this validated package because tenant-specific planning variance and product-specific compliance variance are explicitly handled as runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
